name: sharp
what_it_does: High-performance Node.js image processing library powered by libvips. sharp is 4-5x faster than ImageMagick for common operations (resize, crop, convert) while using less memory. Supports JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, TIFF, GIF, and SVG. Used in agent systems that process user-uploaded images, generate thumbnails, or convert images for ML pipelines.
use_cases: Resize and optimize user-uploaded images in agent APIs before storing or processing with vision models
Convert images to WebP/AVIF for agent-generated content delivery with optimal compression
Generate thumbnail variants of images in agent document processing pipelines
Extract image metadata (dimensions, EXIF, ICC profiles) in agent image analysis workflows
Process large batches of images in agent training data pipelines with streaming pipeline API
not_for: Complex image manipulation (text overlays, advanced compositing) — use ImageMagick or Canvas for complex operations
SVG manipulation — sharp renders SVG to raster but doesn't manipulate SVG DOM
Python-based ML pipelines — use Pillow or OpenCV for Python image processing
best_when: You need fast, memory-efficient image resize/convert/optimize in a Node.js agent backend without ImageMagick overhead.
avoid_when: You need complex image effects, advanced compositing, or PDF processing — sharp is focused on core image I/O operations.

auth.notes: Local image processing library — no authentication.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — limited by CPU and I/O

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear error messages for unsupported formats, invalid dimensions, and pipeline configuration errors. Async errors propagate cleanly through promises.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Image processing pipelines are deterministic — same input and settings always produce same output.
known_agent_gotchas: sharp uses prebuilt native binaries — installation on ARM, musl libc (Alpine), or unusual Node.js versions may fail with no prebuilt binary available
Pipeline is lazy — operations are chained but not executed until .toFile(), .toBuffer(), or .toFormat() is called with await
sharp instances cannot be reused across requests — create a new sharp() instance for each image operation; reusing causes data corruption
Memory: sharp processes images in streaming chunks but keeps intermediate results — very large images (4K+) with multiple operations can spike memory
EXIF rotation: JPEG images may have rotation in EXIF — sharp does NOT auto-rotate by default; use .rotate() to auto-apply EXIF orientation
Animated GIF/WebP: sharp supports animated images in 0.32+ but multi-frame processing requires specific options — check animated() option for frame handling

security_score_components.security_notes: Image parsing vulnerabilities are a historical concern — sharp uses libvips which is regularly patched. Process user-uploaded images in isolated contexts. Validate image dimensions before processing to prevent decompression bombs.
